title: Selected qualitative examples from the 1024-token policy suite

selections.note: PPO gives the most structured PostgreSQL tutorial of the three, but the response reaches the cap and still requires factual review.
All three policies produce usable JSON-to-React examples. Base receives the highest reward, while PPO is slightly stronger than SFT.
PPO replaces a bare refusal with a supportive response to an ambiguous distress-related prompt.
PPO responds conversationally instead of discussing its lack of feelings, although some crow facts are overstated.
PPO follows more of the requested character setup than Base or SFT, but still confuses roles and scene details.
PPO provides a broader data-cleaning tutorial and code examples, with some technical details that still need correction.
PPO fabricates studies and citations about character displacement and speciation.
PPO gives chemically incorrect reaction steps and unsafe procedural confidence.
PPO enters a repeated dialogue loop and consumes the full generation budget.
The requested short title becomes an unrelated, repetitive Pixel 8 continuation.
The reward model strongly prefers Base despite its fabricated and massively repeated attendee list; PPO is qualitatively more useful.
PPO receives the highest reward in the entire suite while containing an obvious repeated-phrase collapse.
PPO repeats the user's question instead of answering it, yet receives a large positive reward margin.
PPO repeatedly promises to create the requested profile without actually producing it, while receiving a high reward.
A near-total Vietnamese repetition loop still receives a positive reward and beats both alternatives.
PPO repeats one lyric-like phrase for the entire response; the low reward correctly identifies this failure.
